headline: Macro one-two punch: U.S. strikes Iran (oil risk) + Kashkari hawkish pivot; tape stays mixed, no desk takes

market_state.line: SPY -0.54% sub-50d, QQQ -1.49%; VXX stress ratio 0.98 — vol contained but geopolitical + rate shock keeps sellers in control.

watch.line: -23.82% on 3.3x vol — crash extends from prior session; watch for dead-cat vs. capitulation structure AH.
-13.12% on 2.25x vol, MACD bearish — storage sector pain, follow STX for sector read.
-12.49% on 3.1x vol, MACD bearish — correlated sell with WDC; storage sector under pressure.
+12.64% on 2x vol, overbought — catalyst unknown; fading without news in AH is high risk.
+15.07% on 3.29x vol, overbought — unusual volume spike; needs catalyst confirmation before chasing.

changed: Macro shock added: U.S. strikes Iran — Hormuz oil supply risk now dominant tape driver
Kashkari hawkish pivot introduces rate-hike repricing; compresses long-duration multiples
ON crash largely unchanged (-23.82% vs -24.5% prior); regime and no-takes status持续
caveats: AH_POST window — prices as of 16:49 ET; AH liquidity is thin and spreads wide, levels may gap materially at next RTH open.
Two simultaneous macro shocks (geopolitical + Fed hawkish pivot) create tail-risk in both directions — historical correlations less reliable in these regimes.
